mechanism_narrative: A rising CPIAUCSL trend with a lagged FEDFUNDS rate below inflation signals falling real rates and easier financial conditions, which supports equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in a majority of months where CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS for two consecutive releases, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above its recent level indicates deteriorating labor demand and weaker household income expectations, which lowers risk appetite and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the average return is not negative in a walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS accelerate together, firms are experiencing stronger sales and payroll demand at the same time, which supports revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-signal portfolio has no positive average 20-day return premium versus baseline, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well and the interaction term is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and tighter earnings conditions for cyclicals, which should pressure the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than half of periods with a 3-month INDPRO decline, or if the rolling 3-year Sharpe of the signal is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp housing-starts downturn indicates broad demand softness and weaker construction-linked cash flows, which foreshadows negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% from its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward returns are nonnegative in a majority of HOUST breakdown episodes, or if the triggered sample does not outperform an unconditional short baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is materially above 4.5% while CPIAUCSL is still elevated, policy is restrictive in real terms and equity discount rates stay high, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes generate positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the interaction adds no incremental explanatory power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, a declining UNRATE and rising PAYEMS together signal labor market repair that improves earnings visibility and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-repair signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive after synchronized UNRATE declines and PAYEMS gains.

mechanism_narrative: During a weak-consumer regime, falling RSAFS relative to GDPC1 indicates that final demand is slowing faster than broad output, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in periods where RSAFS growth trails GDPC1 growth, or if its rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03.
